name: Le Havre
summary: Le Havre is a busy port city on the Normandy coast of France. After it was flattened in World War II it was rebuilt entirely in concrete by architect Auguste Perret, and the whole new city centre is now a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
funFacts: Le Havre's Saint-Joseph Church has a square concrete tower that shoots up 107 metres, and inside thousands of coloured glass squares glow like a giant kaleidoscope.
The whole city centre was rebuilt after 1945 using prefabricated concrete blocks on a giant grid, and UNESCO calls it one of the world's best post-war cities.
Le Volcan, in the middle of town, is a curvy white building shaped a bit like a volcano or a whale, designed by Brazilian architect Oscar Niemeyer.
Just up the coast at Etretat, the white chalk cliffs form huge natural arches and a tall stone needle that pokes 70 metres out of the sea.
Painter Claude Monet grew up in Le Havre, and his painting of the harbour here gave the whole 'Impressionist' art movement its name.
thingsToDo: Climb the steps inside Saint-Joseph's Church and look up at thousands of coloured glass tiles glowing in the tower.
Wander Auguste Perret's concrete city centre and the wide Avenue Foch, sometimes called Le Havre's Champs-Elysees.
Hunt for Monet seascapes inside MuMa, the glass-and-steel modern art museum right by the harbour.
Spot Le Volcan, the curvy white culture centre by Oscar Niemeyer, and peek into its underground library.
Splash and skim stones at Plage du Havre, the long pebbly beach right next to the city.
Take a coach to the Etretat cliffs and walk the clifftop path to see the famous arch and stone needle.

wildlife.name: Red Squirrel
wildlife.description: Bright orange-red squirrel with fluffy ear tufts that scampers through Normandy's woods.

wildlife.name: European Herring Gull
wildlife.description: Big grey-and-white seagull with a yellow beak that yells loudly above the harbour.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Puffin
wildlife.description: Round black-and-white seabird with a colourful striped beak, nesting on nearby chalk cliffs.

wildlife.name: Harbour Seal
wildlife.description: Chubby spotted seal that pops its round head out of the water along the Normandy coast.

localWord.meaning: Hello or good day in French.

images.alt: The tall concrete tower of Saint-Joseph's Church rising over Le Havre's rebuilt city centre.
Le Havre's Hotel de Ville, the city hall designed by Auguste Perret in concrete after WWII.
The glass-and-steel MuMa modern art museum sitting beside the harbour in Le Havre.
Le Volcan, the curvy white cultural centre by Oscar Niemeyer in central Le Havre.
Sunset over the long pebbly Plage du Havre beach beside the city.
The white chalk cliffs and natural Porte d'Aval arch at Etretat in Normandy.
A bright orange-red squirrel with fluffy ear tufts perched on a branch.
A European herring gull standing tall, showing its grey wings and yellow beak.
An Atlantic puffin standing on a clifftop showing its bright striped beak.
A spotted harbour seal poking its round head out of the water.
